sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 32 - Reserve for Immediate Payment of Claims.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 32 - Reserve for Immediate Payment of Claims

observedSectionWindows.note: Immediate-payment reserve guidance, reserve adjustment percentages, and the closing boundary note.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 32 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 33 or later guideline files.
Keep the immediate-payment / reserve-adjustment context explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is encoding-noisy.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Immediate payment of claims reserve guidance
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the two-page window together and do not widen into AG 33.

proposedBatches.title: AG 32 immediate-payment-of-claims reserve guidance slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full two-page guideline as one review-only slice because the immediate-payment reserve context and reserve-adjustment language belong together.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full two-page guideline as one review-only slice because the immediate-payment reserve context and percentage adjustment language belong together.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the reserve-language as regulatory text when the guideline directs how immediate-payment claims reserves should be understood.
reviewStandards.caveatOrCompanionGuidance: Treat the immediate-payment reserve guidance as review-only guidance that should stay separate from any endorsement or product-approval claim.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat immediate payment of claims, curtate reserves, continuous or semi-continuous functions, annual statement, policy year, and proof of death references as terminology or context when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to Section 5 reserve valuation, annual statement instructions, death-claim timing, and AG 33 modifications as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title, scene-setting paragraph, and percentage-table introduction as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1-2 window as a review-only slice and do not split it unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the encoded page, reserve-adjustment examples, and boundary note as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 32 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch must remain review-only by default.
The source posture must stay explicitly active.
Each planned batch must have a matching batch definition.
The AG 32 batch definition file must stay synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag32-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-114 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 32 definition file so the shared runner can find batch-114.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 32 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 32 planning artifact. Planned batch 114 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
